title: Chapter Fifty-Five: Pingyin County
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Three days after the battle on the plain, the three blue-ranked warriors of Radiant Demon Wing reached the southern edge of Shandong Province.

Pingyin County. Their mission target.

"They really left us behind and went," Cheong Myeong said, smiling with the easy amusement of a man who found everything mildly entertaining. "Your sound attack must have been quite reliable, Lightning Flash."

The marketplace was quiet. Stalls laid out on packed earth, goods displayed beneath sagging awnings. The merchants were mostly middle-aged men or elderly women. No young faces. No vitality. Wrinkled skin and dull eyes that held the weariness of people who had endured too much for too long.

Baek Mi-ryeo glanced at the displayed accessories and stroked her long hair.

"Zhangqiu, where the Hwangbo main family sits, borders the Yellow River. If they escape by boat, it becomes troublesome. To secretly control the river approach, we must send minimal personnel to strike the branch families."

"Young men are not visible," Yeon-shin said quietly. "They have been taken for forced labour."

"The Azure Sky Squad and Superintendent's Office confirmed it multiple times. If they were not a martial arts family, the imperial army would have been deployed. It is fortunate -- army deployment would not be good for the people's livelihood either."

Cheong Myeong's voice was calm, but his jaw was tight.

Yeon-shin had considered hiding the golden Hwang mark again. He had thought about it the entire journey to Pingyin County. This was different from previous missions against the Thirteen Evil Sects. The Hwangbo family was a renowned orthodox family. He had wanted to see their true nature with his own eyes.

He saw it now.

A marketplace bled dry of young men. Old women selling trinkets with empty eyes. No fear when three sword-carrying strangers walked among them -- because fear requires the energy to feel it, and these people had none left.

"We can just go to the Hwangbo branch and ask," he said. "What the situation is."

TAK!

Three pairs of feet struck the ground in unison. Lightness technique steps carrying inner power. True qi flooding from meridians to toes, pushing their bodies forward with increasing speed.

The surrounding villagers startled and scrambled backward.

They headed for the largest estate in the village -- a compound of halls without a hanging plaque. No name displayed. But energy waves of internal energy pulsed from within.

HWAK!

Yeon-shin surged ahead of his companions. This was his mission. A grateful opportunity to adapt his sword song to actual combat. He would not disappoint.

KWANG!

The front gate exploded inward. Fragments scattered. The force wave swept the courtyard like a storm.

He stopped.

A drinking party. In the large courtyard before the main hall, men sat on rocks surrounding an ornamental pond decorated with carvings of spirit beasts -- the Four Divine Beasts, qilin, strange creatures with peculiar wings. Wine cups glittered in the afternoon light.

They did not stop drinking.

Five beautifully adorned women sat among the drinkers. One man raised his head from their midst. Handsome. Yellow cornus flowers tucked behind his long ears. Mint-coloured official robes with wide sleeves.

A magistrate. The man appointed by the imperial court to report on the people's livelihood.

Servants glanced at the three warriors standing in the ruins of the gate, then silently returned to their work.

"Today too, our Jinan's heroes have come," the magistrate said, raising his cup high. "The main gate will not last!"

He spoke as if dealing with a very familiar matter.

"Why not just leave Shandong and report to somewhere like the Desolate Sect? I truly cannot understand. With what confidence do these low-level fighters challenge the Hwangbo family?"

Yeon-shin was silent. Behind him, Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo said nothing.

Outside the shattered gate, the marketplace sat hollow and still. Old women with empty eyes. A village stripped of its young.

And here -- wine and women and laughter, in the house of the family that had done the stripping.

The headache pulsed at his crown. Once. Twice.

Yeon-shin gripped the hilt of Northern Light, and felt the blade hum beneath his palm like a living thing eager to sing.
